pattern: The best triple-product inside a subtree only ever needs its three largest values or its two most negative values paired with the single largest value, so carrying just the top three and bottom three seen so far up through a post-order sweep is enough to answer every node without rescanning its whole subtree.
hints: Work from the leaves upward: once you know the three largest and three smallest values inside each child's subtree, you can build the same two short lists for the parent without looking at every node in that subtree again.

A subtree's best product of three values comes from exactly one of two shapes: the three largest values multiplied together, or the two smallest (most negative) values multiplied by the single largest value, since two negatives make a positive.

Merge a node's own value together with the top-three and bottom-three lists carried up from each of its children, then keep only the new top three and bottom three; any subtree smaller than three nodes always gets exactly one coin.

edge_inputs.description: a star-shaped tree where the root's subtree is the whole array of positive costs
a deeper branching tree mixing a negative cost among positives
the only triple available multiplies to a negative number, so the coin count must clamp to zero
the smallest possible tree, two leaves each getting exactly one coin
two large-magnitude negative values paired with the single largest value beats multiplying the three largest values
every candidate value is negative, so every product of three is negative and every coin count clamps to zero
costs pinned to the extreme magnitude allowed by the constraints
a plain chain so every prefix subtree size grows by exactly one node at a time

stress.description: a random tree built by attaching each node to a uniformly random earlier node, with random costs
a long chain (worst case for recursion depth) with random costs

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def placedCoins(self, edges, cost):
        n = len(cost)
        adj = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for a, b in edges:
            adj[a].append(b)
            adj[b].append(a)
        parent = [-1] * n
        order = []
        visited = [False] * n
        stack = [0]
        visited[0] = True
        while stack:
            u = stack.pop()
            order.append(u)
            for v in adj[u]:
                if not visited[v]:
                    visited[v] = True
                    parent[v] = u
                    stack.append(v)
        children = [[] for _ in range(n)]
        for v in range(n):
            if parent[v] != -1:
                children[parent[v]].append(v)
        size = [1] * n
        top3 = [None] * n
        bot3 = [None] * n
        coin = [0] * n
        for u in reversed(order):
            t = [cost[u]]
            b = [cost[u]]
            sz = 1
            for c in children[u]:
                sz += size[c]
                t.extend(top3[c])
                b.extend(bot3[c])
            t.sort(reverse=True)
            del t[3:]
            b.sort()
            del b[3:]
            size[u] = sz
            top3[u] = t
            bot3[u] = b
            if sz < 3:
                coin[u] = 1
            else:
                candidates = []
                if len(t) >= 3:
                    candidates.append(t[0] * t[1] * t[2])
                if len(b) >= 2 and len(t) >= 1:
                    candidates.append(b[0] * b[1] * t[0])
                coin[u] = max(max(candidates), 0)
        return coin
